branding.name: Legal Eyes
branding.disclaimer: The information on this website is for general purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

hero.headline: Justice Through Insight
hero.subtext: Precision-driven legal representation across courts and tribunals. We see what others miss — and we act before others react.

about.title: Clarity in a complex world.
about.description: Legal Eyes is a full-service chambers built around a single principle: the sharpest counsel comes from the sharpest perspective. We pair rigorous legal research with discreet, deeply human advocacy before every forum — from trial courts to the Hon'ble High Court and specialised tribunals.
about.pullQuote: We don't simply argue cases. We architect outcomes.
about.vision: To redefine legal counsel as an act of clarity — replacing noise with insight, and reaction with strategy.

founder.name: Adv Ayush Gupta
founder.note: Two years at the Lucknow HC Bench taught me one thing: every matter is a question of perception. Our work begins where assumptions end.

services.title: Civil Litigation
services.description: Suits for declaration, injunction, specific performance, partition, recovery and title disputes before all civil courts and the Hon'ble High Court.

services.title: Criminal Litigation
services.description: Bail applications, anticipatory bail, FIR quashing under Section 482 CrPC, trial defence, and criminal appeals & revisions.

services.title: Family & Matrimonial
services.description: Divorce, judicial separation, maintenance under Section 125 CrPC, child custody, guardianship, domestic violence and succession matters.

services.title: Consumer Disputes
services.description: Representation before District, State and National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions for deficiency in service and unfair trade practices.

services.title: DRT & Banking
services.description: Proceedings before the Debts Recovery Tribunal (DRT) and DRAT, SARFAESI Act matters, and securitisation appeals.

services.title: Commercial Disputes
services.description: Suits under the Commercial Courts Act, arbitration under the Arbitration & Conciliation Act, 1996, and enforcement of awards.

services.title: Corporate & NCLT
services.description: Company petitions before the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), IBC proceedings, oppression & mismanagement, and corporate advisory.

services.title: Service & Tribunal Matters
services.description: Service disputes before CAT, departmental enquiries, writ petitions under Articles 226 & 227, and matters before specialised tribunals.

services.title: Property & Revenue
services.description: Title verification, conveyancing, RERA complaints, land acquisition, and revenue matters before the Board of Revenue.

whyUs.title: Why discerning clients choose us.
whyUs.description: Two decades of courtroom experience, distilled into a quiet, decisive practice across every forum.

pillars.title: Forum-tested strategy
pillars.description: Every recommendation is informed by precedent and procedural mastery across courts and tribunals.

pillars.title: Senior-led representation
pillars.description: The Advocate-on-Record stays on the brief, from drafting to final arguments.

pillars.title: Discreet & ethical
pillars.description: Confidentiality and professional ethics under the Advocates Act are the floor, not the ceiling, of how we operate.

pillars.title: Pan-forum reach
pillars.description: Active practice before civil, criminal, consumer, family, DRT, NCLT, CAT and the Hon'ble High Court.

insights.title: Insights & Case Notes
insights.description: Long-form thinking on law, regulation, and the art of strategic counsel.

posts.title: Anticipatory bail after Sushila Aggarwal — the new contours
posts.excerpt: How the Constitution Bench reshaped Section 438 CrPC and what it means for accused persons today.

posts.title: Section 9 vs Section 17: choosing your interim forum
posts.excerpt: When to approach the Court and when to invoke the Arbitral Tribunal for urgent interim reliefs.

posts.title: Pecuniary jurisdiction after the 2019 Act
posts.excerpt: A practitioner's note on choosing between District, State and National Commissions.

posts.title: SARFAESI possession: the borrower's playbook
posts.excerpt: Procedural safeguards under Section 13(4) and the route to the Debts Recovery Tribunal.

posts.title: Maintenance pendente lite — quantum & enforcement
posts.excerpt: Reading Rajnesh v. Neha into everyday matrimonial practice.

posts.title: Order VII Rule 11 — the early exit door
posts.excerpt: Strategic rejection of plaints and how appellate courts are reading the rule today.

team.title: Counsel you'll recognise.
team.description: A small bench of seasoned practitioners — chosen for judgment, not headcount.

contact.title: Begin a conversation.
contact.description: Tell us about your matter. Counsel will respond within one working day.
